links.title: V_1: Unifying Generation and Self-Verification for Parallel Reasoners

primary_link.title: V_1: Unifying Generation and Self-Verification for Parallel Reasoners

papers.summary: Test-time scaling for complex reasoning tasks shows that leveraging inference-time compute, by methods such as independently sampling and aggregating multiple solutions, results in significantly better task outcomes. However, a critical bottleneck is verification: sampling is only effective if correct solutions can be reliably identified among candidates. While existing approaches typically evaluate candidates independently via scalar scoring, we demonstrate that models are substantially stronger at pairwise self-verification. Leveraging this insight, we introduce V_1, a framework that unifies generation and verification through efficient pairwise ranking. V_1 comprises two components: V_1-Infer, an uncertainty-guided algorithm using a tournament-based ranking that dynamically allocates self-verification compute to candidate pairs whose relative correctness is most uncertain; and V_1-PairRL, an RL framework that jointly trains a single model as both generator and pairwise self-verifier, ensuring the verifier adapts to the generator's evolving distribution. On code generation (LiveCodeBench, CodeContests, SWE-Bench) and math reasoning (AIME, HMMT) benchmarks, V_1-Infer improves Pass@1 by up to 10% over pointwise verification and outperforms recent test-time scaling methods while being significantly more efficient. Furthermore, V_1-PairRL achieves 7--9% test-time scaling gains over standard RL and pointwise joint training, and improves base Pass@1 by up to 8.7% over standard RL in a code-generation setting.
papers.summary_zh: 複雜推理任務的測試時擴展 (Test-time scaling) 表明，透過諸如獨立採樣和聚合多個解決方案等方法來利用推論時運算 (inference-time compute)，可以顯著改善任務結果。然而，一個關鍵瓶頸是驗證：採樣只有在候選方案中能夠可靠地識別出正確的解決方案時才有效。儘管現有方法通常透過標量評分獨立評估候選方案，但我們證明模型在成對自驗證 (pairwise self-verification) 方面顯著更強。利用這一洞察，我們引入了 V_1，一個透過高效成對排序統一生成和驗證的框架。V_1 包含兩個組件：V_1-Infer，一個使用基於錦標賽排序 (tournament-based ranking) 的不確定性引導演算法，它動態地將自驗證運算分配給相對正確性最不確定的候選對；以及 V_1-PairRL，一個 RL 框架，它共同訓練一個模型作為生成器和成對自驗證器，確保驗證器適應生成器不斷演變的分佈。在程式碼生成 (LiveCodeBench, CodeContests, SWE-Bench) 和數學推理 (AIME, HMMT) 基準測試中，V_1-Infer 相較於點對點驗證 (pointwise verification) 將 Pass@1 提高了多達 10%，並且超越了最近的測試時擴展方法，同時效率顯著更高。此外，V_1-PairRL 相較於標準 RL 和點對點聯合訓練 (pointwise joint training) 實現了 7-9% 的測試時擴展增益，並在程式碼生成設定中相較於標準 RL 將基礎 Pass@1 提高了多達 8.7%。
papers.title: V_1: Unifying Generation and Self-Verification for Parallel Reasoners
papers.title_zh: V_1：為並行推理器統一生成與自驗證

links.title: EmbodiedSplat: Online Feed-Forward Semantic 3DGS for Open-Vocabulary 3D Scene Understanding

primary_link.title: EmbodiedSplat: Online Feed-Forward Semantic 3DGS for Open-Vocabulary 3D Scene Understanding

papers.summary: Understanding a 3D scene immediately with its exploration is essential for embodied tasks, where an agent must construct and comprehend the 3D scene in an online and nearly real-time manner. In this study, we propose EmbodiedSplat, an online feed-forward 3DGS for open-vocabulary scene understanding that enables simultaneous online 3D reconstruction and 3D semantic understanding from the streaming images. Unlike existing open-vocabulary 3DGS methods which are typically restricted to either offline or per-scene optimization setting, our objectives are two-fold: 1) Reconstructs the semantic-embedded 3DGS of the entire scene from over 300 streaming images in an online manner. 2) Highly generalizable to novel scenes with feed-forward design and supports nearly real-time 3D semantic reconstruction when combined with real-time 2D models. To achieve these objectives, we propose an Online Sparse Coefficients Field with a CLIP Global Codebook where it binds the 2D CLIP embeddings to each 3D Gaussian while minimizing memory consumption and preserving the full semantic generalizability of CLIP. Furthermore, we generate 3D geometric-aware CLIP features by aggregating the partial point cloud of 3DGS through 3D U-Net to compensate the 3D geometric prior to 2D-oriented language embeddings. Extensive experiments on diverse indoor datasets, including ScanNet, ScanNet++, and Replica, demonstrate both the effectiveness and efficiency of our method. Check out our project page in https://0nandon.github.io/EmbodiedSplat/.
papers.summary_zh: 在探索 3D 場景時即時理解它對於 Embodied tasks 至關重要，其中 an agent 必須以線上和近乎即時的方式構建和理解 3D 場景。在本研究中，我們提出了 EmbodiedSplat，一個用於開放詞彙場景理解的線上前饋 3DGS，它能夠從串流圖像中同時進行線上 3D reconstruction 和 3D semantic understanding。與現有通常受限於離線或每場景優化設置的 open-vocabulary 3DGS 方法不同，我們的目標有兩個：1) 從超過 300 張串流圖像中以線上方式重建整個場景的 semantic-embedded 3DGS。2) 透過 feed-forward 設計對新場景具有高度泛化能力，並結合 real-time 2D models 時支援近乎即時的 3D semantic reconstruction。為了實現這些目標，我們提出了一個帶有 CLIP Global Codebook 的 Online Sparse Coefficients Field，它將 2D CLIP embeddings 綁定到每個 3D Gaussian，同時最大程度地減少記憶體消耗並保留 CLIP 的完整語義泛化能力。此外，我們透過 3D U-Net 聚合 3DGS 的 partial point cloud 來生成 3D geometric-aware CLIP features，以彌補 2D-oriented language embeddings 的 3D 幾何先驗。在 ScanNet、ScanNet++ 和 Replica 等多樣化室內數據集上的廣泛實驗證明了我們方法的有效性和效率。請訪問我們的專案頁面：https://0nandon.github.io/EmbodiedSplat/。

links.title: DARE: Aligning LLM Agents with the R Statistical Ecosystem via Distribution-Aware Retrieval

primary_link.title: DARE: Aligning LLM Agents with the R Statistical Ecosystem via Distribution-Aware Retrieval

papers.summary: Large Language Model (LLM) agents can automate data-science workflows, but many rigorous statistical methods implemented in R remain underused because LLMs struggle with statistical knowledge and tool retrieval. Existing retrieval-augmented approaches focus on function-level semantics and ignore data distribution, producing suboptimal matches. We propose DARE (Distribution-Aware Retrieval Embedding), a lightweight, plug-and-play retrieval model that incorporates data distribution information into function representations for R package retrieval. Our main contributions are: (i) RPKB, a curated R Package Knowledge Base derived from 8,191 high-quality CRAN packages; (ii) DARE, an embedding model that fuses distributional features with function metadata to improve retrieval relevance; and (iii) RCodingAgent, an R-oriented LLM agent for reliable R code generation and a suite of statistical analysis tasks for systematically evaluating LLM agents in realistic analytical scenarios. Empirically, DARE achieves an NDCG at 10 of 93.47%, outperforming state-of-the-art open-source embedding models by up to 17% on package retrieval while using substantially fewer parameters. Integrating DARE into RCodingAgent yields significant gains on downstream analysis tasks. This work helps narrow the gap between LLM automation and the mature R statistical ecosystem.

papers.summary: Multimodal web agents that process both screenshots and accessibility trees are increasingly deployed to interact with web interfaces, yet their dual-stream architecture opens an underexplored attack surface: an adversary who injects content into the webpage DOM simultaneously corrupts both observation channels with a consistent deceptive narrative. Our vulnerability analysis on MiniWob++ reveals that attacks including a visual component far outperform text-only injections, exposing critical gap
papers.summary_zh: 處理螢幕截圖和 accessibility trees 的 Multimodal web agents 正被越來越多地部署以與網路介面互動，然而其 dual-stream architecture 開闢了一個未充分探索的攻擊面：將內容注入網頁 DOM 的攻擊者會同時用一致的欺騙性敘述破壞兩個觀察通道。我們對 MiniWob++ 的漏洞分析顯示，包含視覺元件的攻擊遠優於僅限文字的注入，暴露了關鍵的缺口。
papers.title: Dual-Modality Multi-Stage Adversarial Safety Training: Robustifying Multimodal Web Agents Against Cross-Modal Attacks
papers.title_zh: 雙模態多階段對抗安全訓練：強化多模態 Web Agents 以防範跨模態攻擊

links.title: Efficient Refusal Ablation in LLM through Optimal Transport

primary_link.title: Efficient Refusal Ablation in LLM through Optimal Transport

papers.summary: Safety-aligned language models refuse harmful requests through learned refusal behaviors encoded in their internal representations. Recent activation-based jailbreaking methods circumvent these safety mechanisms by applying orthogonal projections to remove refusal directions, but these approaches treat refusal as a one-dimensional phenomenon and ignore the rich distributional structure of model activations. We introduce a principled framework based on optimal transport theory that transforms the
papers.summary_zh: Safety-aligned language models 透過編碼在其內部表示中的學習到的 refusal behaviors 拒絕有害請求。最近基於 activation 的 jailbreaking 方法透過應用 orthogonal projections 來移除 refusal directions，從而規避了這些安全機制，但這些方法將拒絕視為一維現象，並忽略了模型 activations 豐富的分佈結構。我們引入了一個基於 optimal transport theory 的原則性框架，它轉換了...
papers.title: Efficient Refusal Ablation in LLM through Optimal Transport
papers.title_zh: 透過 Optimal Transport 在 LLM 中實現高效拒絕消融

links.title: Model Medicine: A Clinical Framework for Understanding, Diagnosing, and Treating AI Models

primary_link.title: Model Medicine: A Clinical Framework for Understanding, Diagnosing, and Treating AI Models

papers.summary: Model Medicine is the science of understanding, diagnosing, treating, and preventing disorders in AI models, grounded in the principle that AI models -- like biological organisms -- have internal structures, dynamic processes, heritable traits, observable symptoms, classifiable conditions, and treatable states. This paper introduces Model Medicine as a research program, bridging the gap between current AI interpretability research (anatomical observation) and the systematic clinical practice tha
papers.summary_zh: Model Medicine 是一門理解、診斷、治療和預防 AI 模型中各種障礙的科學，其基礎原則是 AI 模型——如同生物有機體——具有內部結構、動態過程、可遺傳的特性、可觀察的症狀、可分類的狀況和可治療的狀態。本文介紹了 Model Medicine 作為一個研究計畫，彌合了當前 AI interpretability research (anatomical observation) 與系統性臨床實踐之間的差距。
papers.title: Model Medicine: A Clinical Framework for Understanding, Diagnosing, and Treating AI Models
papers.title_zh: 模型醫學：一個用於理解、診斷和治療 AI 模型的臨床框架

links.title: HDLFORGE: A Two-Stage Multi-Agent Framework for Efficient Verilog Code Generation with Adaptive Model Escalation

primary_link.title: HDLFORGE: A Two-Stage Multi-Agent Framework for Efficient Verilog Code Generation with Adaptive Model Escalation

radar.summary: We present HDLFORGE, a two-stage multi-agent framework for automated Verilog generation that optimizes the trade-off between generation speed and accuracy. The system uses a compact coder with a medium-sized LLM by default (Stage A) and escalates to a stronger coder with an ultra-large LLM (Stage B) only when needed, guided by a calibrated score from inexpensive diagnostics including compilation, lint, and smoke tests. A key innovation is a counterexample-guided formal agent that converts bounde
radar.summary_zh: 我們提出了 HDLFORGE，這是一個用於自動化 Verilog 生成的兩階段多代理框架，它優化了生成速度和準確性之間的權衡。該系統預設使用帶有中型 LLM 的緊湊型編碼器（階段 A），並僅在需要時，在來自包括 compilation、lint 和 smoke tests 等低成本診斷的校準分數引導下，升級到帶有超大型 LLM 的更強大編碼器（階段 B）。一個關鍵的創新是一個反例引導的形式化代理，它將 bounde...
radar.title: HDLFORGE: A Two-Stage Multi-Agent Framework for Efficient Verilog Code Generation with Adaptive Model Escalation
radar.title_zh: HDLFORGE：一個用於高效 Verilog 程式碼生成的兩階段多代理框架，具有自適應模型升級

links.title: MPCEval: A Benchmark for Multi-Party Conversation Generation

primary_link.title: MPCEval: A Benchmark for Multi-Party Conversation Generation

radar.summary: Multi-party conversation generation, such as smart reply and collaborative assistants, is an increasingly important capability of generative AI, yet its evaluation remains a critical bottleneck. Compared to two-party dialogue, multi-party settings introduce distinct challenges, including complex turn-taking, role-dependent speaker behavior, long-range conversational structure, and multiple equally valid continuations. Accordingly, we introduce MPCEval, a task-aware evaluation and benchmarking su
radar.summary_zh: 多方對話生成，例如 smart reply 和 collaborative assistants，是生成式 AI 越來越重要的能力，但其評估仍然是一個關鍵瓶頸。與兩方對話相比，多方設定帶來了獨特的挑戰，包括複雜的 turn-taking、依賴角色的 speaker behavior、long-range conversational structure 以及多個同樣有效的 continuations。因此，我們引入了 MPCEval，這是一個 task-aware evaluation 和 benchmarking su...
radar.title: MPCEval: A Benchmark for Multi-Party Conversation Generation
radar.title_zh: MPCEval：一個用於多方對話生成的基準

links.title: Core-based Hierarchies for Efficient GraphRAG

primary_link.title: Core-based Hierarchies for Efficient GraphRAG

radar.summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances large language models by incorporating external knowledge. However, existing vector-based methods often fail on global sensemaking tasks that require reasoning across many documents. GraphRAG addresses this by organizing documents into a knowledge graph with hierarchical communities that can be recursively summarized. Current GraphRAG approaches rely on Leiden clustering for community detection, but we prove that on sparse knowledge graphs, where ave
radar.summary_zh: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) 通過整合外部知識來增強大型語言模型。然而，現有的基於向量的方法在需要跨多個文件進行推理的 global sensemaking tasks 上常常表現不佳。GraphRAG 通過將文件組織成具有可以遞歸總結的層次社群的知識圖譜來解決這個問題。當前的 GraphRAG 方法依賴於 Leiden clustering 進行社群檢測，但我們證明在 sparse knowledge graphs 上，其中 ave...
radar.title: Core-based Hierarchies for Efficient GraphRAG
radar.title_zh: 用於高效 GraphRAG 的基於核心的層次結構

links.title: Wiki-R1: Incentivizing Multimodal Reasoning for Knowledge-based VQA via Data and Sampling Curriculum

primary_link.title: Wiki-R1: Incentivizing Multimodal Reasoning for Knowledge-based VQA via Data and Sampling Curriculum

radar.summary: Knowledge-Based Visual Question Answering (KB-VQA) requires models to answer questions about an image by integrating external knowledge, posing significant challenges due to noisy retrieval and the structured, encyclopedic nature of the knowledge base. These characteristics create a distributional gap from pretrained multimodal large language models (MLLMs), making effective reasoning and domain adaptation difficult in the post-training stage. In this work, we propose \textit{Wiki-R1}, a data-ge
radar.summary_zh: Knowledge-Based Visual Question Answering (KB-VQA) 要求模型通過整合外部知識來回答關於圖像的問題，由於 noisy retrieval 以及知識庫的結構化、百科全書式性質而帶來重大挑戰。這些特性與預訓練 multimodal large language models (MLLMs) 產生了 distributional gap，使得在 post-training stage 難以進行有效的推理和 domain adaptation。在這項工作中，我們提出了 Wiki-R1，這是一個數據驅...
radar.title: Wiki-R1: Incentivizing Multimodal Reasoning for Knowledge-based VQA via Data and Sampling Curriculum
radar.title_zh: Wiki-R1：通過數據和採樣課程激勵基於知識的 VQA 的多模態推理

links.title: Recursive Inference Machines for Neural Reasoning

primary_link.title: Recursive Inference Machines for Neural Reasoning

radar.summary: Neural reasoners such as Tiny Recursive Models (TRMs) solve complex problems by combining neural backbones with specialized inference schemes. Such inference schemes have been a central component of stochastic reasoning systems, where inference rules are applied to a stochastic model to derive answers to complex queries. In this work, we bridge these two paradigms by introducing Recursive Inference Machines (RIMs), a neural reasoning framework that explicitly incorporates recursive inference mec
radar.summary_zh: 神經推理器，例如 Tiny Recursive Models (TRMs)，通過將 neural backbones 與 specialized inference schemes 相結合來解決複雜問題。這些 inference schemes 一直是 stochastic reasoning systems 的核心組成部分，在這些系統中，推理規則應用於 stochastic model 以得出複雜查詢的答案。在這項工作中，我們通過引入 Recursive Inference Machines (RIMs) 來彌合這兩種範式，這是一個明確包含 recursive inference mec...
radar.title: Recursive Inference Machines for Neural Reasoning
radar.title_zh: 用於神經推理的遞歸推理機

links.title: Jagarin: A Three-Layer Architecture for Hibernating Personal Duty Agents on Mobile

primary_link.title: Jagarin: A Three-Layer Architecture for Hibernating Personal Duty Agents on Mobile

radar.summary: Personal AI agents face a fundamental deployment paradox on mobile: persistent background execution drains battery and violates platform sandboxing policies, yet purely reactive agents miss time-sensitive obligations until the user remembers to ask. We present Jagarin, a three-layer architecture that resolves this paradox through structured hibernation and demand-driven wake. The first layer, DAWN (Duty-Aware Wake Network), is an on-device heuristic engine that computes a composite urgency score
radar.summary_zh: 個人 AI agents 在行動裝置上面臨一個根本的部署悖論：持久的背景執行會耗盡電池並違反平台 sandboxing 策略，而純粹反應式的 agents 則會錯過時間敏感的任務，直到使用者記得詢問為止。我們提出了 Jagarin，一種透過結構化休眠和需求驅動喚醒來解決此悖論的三層架構。第一層，DAWN (Duty-Aware Wake Network)，是一個裝置上的 heuristic engine，用於計算綜合緊急性分數。
radar.title: Jagarin: A Three-Layer Architecture for Hibernating Personal Duty Agents on Mobile
radar.title_zh: Jagarin：一種用於在行動裝置上休眠個人任務代理的三層架構

links.title: AegisUI: Behavioral Anomaly Detection for Structured User Interface Protocols in AI Agent Systems

primary_link.title: AegisUI: Behavioral Anomaly Detection for Structured User Interface Protocols in AI Agent Systems

radar.summary:
AI agents that build user interfaces on the fly assembling buttons, forms, and data displays from structured protocol payloads are becoming common in production systems. The trouble is that a payload can pass every schema check and still trick a user: a button might say "View invoice" while its hidden action wipes an account, or a display widget might quietly bind to an internal salary field. Current defenses stop at syntax; they were never built to catch this kind of behavioral mismatch.
  We b
radar.summary_zh: 在生產系統中，AI agents 透過組裝按鈕、表單和資料顯示來即時建構 user interfaces，這些都來自結構化 protocol payloads，這種情況正變得越來越普遍。問題在於，一個 payload 即使通過了所有 schema check，仍然可能欺騙使用者：一個按鈕可能顯示「View invoice」，但其隱藏動作卻是清除帳戶，或者一個 display widget 可能悄悄地綁定到內部薪資欄位。目前的防禦僅止於 syntax；它們從未被設計來捕捉這種 behavioral mismatch。
radar.title: AegisUI: Behavioral Anomaly Detection for Structured User Interface Protocols in AI Agent Systems
radar.title_zh: AegisUI：AI Agent 系統中結構化使用者介面協定的行為異常偵測

links.title: ThaiSafetyBench: Assessing Language Model Safety in Thai Cultural Contexts

primary_link.title: ThaiSafetyBench: Assessing Language Model Safety in Thai Cultural Contexts

radar.summary: The safety evaluation of large language models (LLMs) remains largely centered on English, leaving non-English languages and culturally grounded risks underexplored. In this work, we investigate LLM safety in the context of the Thai language and culture and introduce ThaiSafetyBench, an open-source benchmark comprising 1,954 malicious prompts written in Thai. The dataset covers both general harmful prompts and attacks that are explicitly grounded in Thai cultural, social, and contextual nuances.
radar.summary_zh: large language models (LLMs) 的安全性評估在很大程度上仍以英語為中心，導致非英語語言和基於文化的風險研究不足。在這項工作中，我們在泰語和泰國文化的背景下調查 LLM 的安全性，並推出了 ThaiSafetyBench，這是一個開源的 benchmark，包含 1,954 個以泰語編寫的惡意 prompts。該 dataset 涵蓋了一般的有害 prompts 以及明確基於泰國文化、社會和情境細微差別的攻擊。
radar.title: ThaiSafetyBench: Assessing Language Model Safety in Thai Cultural Contexts
radar.title_zh: ThaiSafetyBench：評估泰語文化背景下的語言模型安全

links.title: A Simple Baseline for Unifying Understanding, Generation, and Editing via Vanilla Next-token Prediction

primary_link.title: A Simple Baseline for Unifying Understanding, Generation, and Editing via Vanilla Next-token Prediction

radar.summary: In this work, we introduce Wallaroo, a simple autoregressive baseline that leverages next-token prediction to unify multi-modal understanding, image generation, and editing at the same time. Moreover, Wallaroo supports multi-resolution image input and output, as well as bilingual support for both Chinese and English. We decouple the visual encoding into separate pathways and apply a four-stage training strategy to reshape the model's capabilities. Experiments are conducted on various benchmarks
radar.summary_zh: 在這項工作中，我們介紹了 Wallaroo，一個簡單的 autoregressive baseline，它利用 next-token prediction 同時統一了 multi-modal understanding、image generation 和 editing。此外，Wallaroo 支援 multi-resolution image input 和 output，以及中文和英文的雙語支援。我們將 visual encoding 解耦為獨立的 pathways，並採用四階段 training strategy 來重塑模型的 capabilities。實驗在各種 benchmarks 上進行。
radar.title: A Simple Baseline for Unifying Understanding, Generation, and Editing via Vanilla Next-token Prediction
radar.title_zh: 一種透過 Vanilla Next-token Prediction 統一理解、生成和編輯的簡單基準模型

links.title: VisionPangu: A Compact and Fine-Grained Multimodal Assistant with 1.7B Parameters

primary_link.title: VisionPangu: A Compact and Fine-Grained Multimodal Assistant with 1.7B Parameters

radar.summary: Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) have achieved strong performance in vision-language understanding, yet many existing approaches rely on large-scale architectures and coarse supervision, which limits their ability to generate detailed image captions. In this work, we present VisionPangu, a compact 1.7B-parameter multimodal model designed to improve detailed image captioning through efficient multimodal alignment and high-quality supervision. Our model combines an InternVL-derived vision encoder wi
radar.summary_zh: Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) 在 vision-language understanding 方面取得了強勁的表現，但許多現有方法依賴於大型架構和 coarse supervision，這限制了它們生成詳細 image captions 的能力。在這項工作中，我們提出了 VisionPangu，一個緊湊的 1.7B-parameter multimodal model，旨在透過高效的 multimodal alignment 和高質量的 supervision 來改進詳細的 image captioning。我們的模型結合了一個 InternVL-derived vision encoder。
radar.title: VisionPangu: A Compact and Fine-Grained Multimodal Assistant with 1.7B Parameters
radar.title_zh: VisionPangu：一個具有 1.7B 參數的緊湊型細粒度多模態助手

links.title: BandPO: Bridging Trust Regions and Ratio Clipping via Probability-Aware Bounds for LLM Reinforcement Learning

primary_link.title: BandPO: Bridging Trust Regions and Ratio Clipping via Probability-Aware Bounds for LLM Reinforcement Learning

radar.summary: Proximal constraints are fundamental to the stability of the Large Language Model reinforcement learning. While the canonical clipping mechanism in PPO serves as an efficient surrogate for trust regions, we identify a critical bottleneck: fixed bounds strictly constrain the upward update margin of low-probability actions, disproportionately suppressing high-advantage tail strategies and inducing rapid entropy collapse. To address this, we introduce Band-constrained Policy Optimization (BandPO).
radar.summary_zh: 近端約束對於 Large Language Model 強化學習的穩定性至關重要。雖然 PPO 中典型的 clipping 機制作為 trust regions 的有效替代，但我們發現一個關鍵瓶頸：固定邊界嚴格限制了低機率動作的向上更新裕度，不成比例地抑制了高優勢的尾部策略，並導致了快速的 entropy collapse。為解決此問題，我們引入了 Band-constrained Policy Optimization (BandPO)。
radar.title: BandPO: Bridging Trust Regions and Ratio Clipping via Probability-Aware Bounds for LLM Reinforcement Learning
radar.title_zh: BandPO：透過機率感知邊界彌合信任區域與比例裁剪，應用於 LLM 強化學習

links.title: How Does the ReLU Activation Affect the Implicit Bias of Gradient Descent on High-dimensional Neural Network Regression?

primary_link.title: How Does the ReLU Activation Affect the Implicit Bias of Gradient Descent on High-dimensional Neural Network Regression?

radar.summary: Overparameterized ML models, including neural networks, typically induce underdetermined training objectives with multiple global minima. The implicit bias refers to the limiting global minimum that is attained by a common optimization algorithm, such as gradient descent (GD). In this paper, we characterize the implicit bias of GD for training a shallow ReLU model with the squared loss on high-dimensional random features. Prior work showed that the implicit bias does not exist in the worst-case
radar.summary_zh: 過參數化 (Overparameterized) 的 ML 模型，包括 neural networks，通常會產生具有多個 global minima 的欠定 (underdetermined) 訓練目標。Implicit bias 指的是由常見最佳化演算法（例如 Gradient Descent (GD)）所達到的極限 global minimum。在本文中，我們描述了 GD 在高維 random features 上，使用 squared loss 訓練淺層 ReLU 模型的 implicit bias。先前的工作表明，在最壞情況下，implicit bias 並不存在。
radar.title: How Does the ReLU Activation Affect the Implicit Bias of Gradient Descent on High-dimensional Neural Network Regression?
radar.title_zh: ReLU 啟用函數如何影響高維神經網路回歸中 Gradient Descent 的隱式偏差？

links.title: Causally Robust Reward Learning from Reason-Augmented Preference Feedback

primary_link.title: Causally Robust Reward Learning from Reason-Augmented Preference Feedback

radar.summary: Preference-based reward learning is widely used for shaping agent behavior to match a user's preference, yet its sparse binary feedback makes it especially vulnerable to causal confusion. The learned reward often latches onto spurious features that merely co-occur with preferred trajectories during training, collapsing when those correlations disappear or reverse at test time. We introduce ReCouPLe, a lightweight framework that uses natural language rationales to provide the missing causal signa
radar.summary_zh: Preference-based reward learning 被廣泛應用於塑造 agent 行為以符合使用者偏好，然而其稀疏的二元回饋使其特別容易受到 causal confusion 的影響。學習到的 reward 通常會附著於在訓練期間僅與首選軌跡共同發生的 spurious features，當這些關聯在測試時消失或反轉時便會崩潰。我們引入 ReCouPLe，這是一個輕量級的框架，它使用 natural language rationales 來提供缺失的 causal signal。
radar.title: Causally Robust Reward Learning from Reason-Augmented Preference Feedback
radar.title_zh: 從理性增強偏好回饋中進行因果穩健的獎勵學習

links.title: Breaking Contextual Inertia: Reinforcement Learning with Single-Turn Anchors for Stable Multi-Turn Interaction

primary_link.title: Breaking Contextual Inertia: Reinforcement Learning with Single-Turn Anchors for Stable Multi-Turn Interaction

radar.summary: While LLMs demonstrate strong reasoning capabilities when provided with full information in a single turn, they exhibit substantial vulnerability in multi-turn interactions. Specifically, when information is revealed incrementally or requires updates, models frequently fail to integrate new constraints, leading to a collapse in performance compared to their single-turn baselines. We term the root cause as \emph{Contextual Inertia}: a phenomenon where models rigidly adhere to previous reasoning t
radar.summary_zh: 儘管 LLMs 在單輪提供完整資訊時展現出強大的推理能力，但它們在多輪互動中卻表現出顯著的脆弱性。具體來說，當資訊是增量揭示或需要更新時，模型經常未能整合新的約束，導致其性能相較於 single-turn baselines 崩潰。我們將其根本原因稱為 \emph{Contextual Inertia}：一種模型僵化地遵循先前推理的現象。
radar.title: Breaking Contextual Inertia: Reinforcement Learning with Single-Turn Anchors for Stable Multi-Turn Interaction
radar.title_zh: 打破語境慣性：使用 Single-Turn Anchors 進行強化學習以實現穩定的多輪互動

links.title: TSEmbed: Unlocking Task Scaling in Universal Multimodal Embeddings

primary_link.title: TSEmbed: Unlocking Task Scaling in Universal Multimodal Embeddings

radar.summary: Despite the exceptional reasoning capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), their adaptation into universal embedding models is significantly impeded by task conflict. To address this, we propose TSEmbed, a universal multimodal embedding framework that synergizes Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) with Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to explicitly disentangle conflicting task objectives. Moreover, we introduce Expert-Aware Negative Sampling (EANS), a novel strategy that leverages expert routing
radar.summary_zh: 儘管 Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) 具有卓越的推理能力，但它們轉換為通用 embedding models 的適應性卻因 task conflict 而受到嚴重阻礙。為解決此問題，我們提出了 TSEmbed，這是一個通用的 multimodal embedding 框架，它將 Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) 與 Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) 結合，以明確地 disentangle 相互衝突的 task objectives。此外，我們引入了 Expert-Aware Negative Sampling (EANS)，這是一種利用 expert routing 的新穎策略。
radar.title: TSEmbed: Unlocking Task Scaling in Universal Multimodal Embeddings
radar.title_zh: TSEmbed：釋放通用多模態嵌入中的任務擴展潛力

links.title: Evaluating the Search Agent in a Parallel World

primary_link.title: Evaluating the Search Agent in a Parallel World

radar.summary: Integrating web search tools has significantly extended the capability of LLMs to address open-world, real-time, and long-tail problems. However, evaluating these Search Agents presents formidable challenges. First, constructing high-quality deep search benchmarks is prohibitively expensive, while unverified synthetic data often suffers from unreliable sources. Second, static benchmarks face dynamic obsolescence: as internet information evolves, complex queries requiring deep research often degr
radar.summary_zh: 整合 web search tools 已顯著擴展 LLMs 處理 open-world、real-time 和 long-tail problems 的能力。然而，評估這些 Search Agents 卻帶來了巨大的挑戰。首先，構建高品質的 deep search benchmarks 的成本過高，而未經證實的 synthetic data 往往來源不可靠。其次，static benchmarks 面臨動態過時的問題：隨著網際網路資訊的演變，需要深度研究的複雜 queries 往往會降級。
radar.title: Evaluating the Search Agent in a Parallel World
radar.title_zh: 評估平行世界中的 Search Agent

links.title: Decoding the Pulse of Reasoning VLMs in Multi-Image Understanding Tasks

primary_link.title: Decoding the Pulse of Reasoning VLMs in Multi-Image Understanding Tasks

radar.summary: Multi-image reasoning remains a significant challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). We investigate a previously overlooked phenomenon: during chain-of-thought (CoT) generation, the text-to-image (T2I) attention of reasoning VLMs exhibits diffuse "pulses": sporadic and unfocused attention patterns that fail to concentrate on task-relevant images. We further reveal a systematic positional bias in attention allocation across images. Motivated by these observations, we propose PulseFocus, a tra
radar.summary_zh: 多影像推理對於 vision-language models (VLMs) 來說仍然是一個重大挑戰。我們研究了一個先前被忽視的現象：在 chain-of-thought (CoT) 生成過程中，推理 VLMs 的 text-to-image (T2I) attention 呈現出擴散性的「脈衝」：零星且不集中的 attention patterns，未能集中於與任務相關的影像。我們進一步揭示了影像間 attention allocation 的系統性位置偏差。受這些觀察結果啟發，我們提出了 PulseFocus，這是一種訓練方法。
radar.title: Decoding the Pulse of Reasoning VLMs in Multi-Image Understanding Tasks

links.title: Neuro-Symbolic Financial Reasoning via Deterministic Fact Ledgers and Adversarial Low-Latency Hallucination Detector

primary_link.title: Neuro-Symbolic Financial Reasoning via Deterministic Fact Ledgers and Adversarial Low-Latency Hallucination Detector

radar.summary: Standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures fail in high-stakes financial domains due to two fundamental limitations: the inherent arithmetic incompetence of Large Language Models (LLMs) and the distributional semantic conflation of dense vector retrieval (e.g., mapping ``Net Income'' to ``Net Sales'' due to contextual proximity). In deterministic domains, a 99% accuracy rate yields 0% operational trust. To achieve zero-hallucination financial reasoning, we introduce the Verifiab
radar.summary_zh: 標準的 Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) 架構在高風險的金融領域中會失效，原因在於兩個基本限制：Large Language Models (LLMs) 固有的算術無能，以及 dense vector retrieval 的分佈式語義混淆（例如，由於上下文鄰近性將「Net Income」映射到「Net Sales」）。在確定性領域中，99% 的準確度會導致 0% 的 operational trust。為了實現 zero-hallucination financial reasoning，我們引入了 Verifiab。
radar.title: Neuro-Symbolic Financial Reasoning via Deterministic Fact Ledgers and Adversarial Low-Latency Hallucination Detector
radar.title_zh: 透過 Deterministic Fact Ledgers 和 Adversarial Low-Latency Hallucination Detector 實現神經符號金融推理

links.title: Coordinated Semantic Alignment and Evidence Constraints for Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Large Language Models

primary_link.title: Coordinated Semantic Alignment and Evidence Constraints for Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Large Language Models

radar.summary: Retrieval augmented generation mitigates limitations of large language models in factual consistency and knowledge updating by introducing external knowledge. However, practical applications still suffer from semantic misalignment between retrieved results and generation objectives, as well as insufficient evidence utilization. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a retrieval augmented generation method that integrates semantic alignment with evidence constraints through coordinated
radar.summary_zh: Retrieval augmented generation 透過引入外部知識，彌補了 Large Language Models 在 factual consistency 和 knowledge updating 方面的限制。然而，實際應用仍存在檢索結果與生成目標之間的 semantic misalignment，以及證據利用不足的問題。為解決這些挑戰，本文提出了一種 retrieval augmented generation 方法，透過協調方式整合 semantic alignment 與 evidence constraints。
radar.title: Coordinated Semantic Alignment and Evidence Constraints for Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Large Language Models
radar.title_zh: 針對結合 Large Language Models 的 Retrieval-Augmented Generation 協調語義對齊和證據約束

links.title: Progressive Refinement Regulation for Accelerating Diffusion Language Model Decoding

primary_link.title: Progressive Refinement Regulation for Accelerating Diffusion Language Model Decoding

radar.summary: Diffusion language models generate text through iterative denoising under a uniform refinement rule applied to all tokens. However, tokens stabilize at different rates in practice, leading to substantial redundant refinement and motivating refinement control over the denoising process. Existing approaches typically assess refinement necessity from instantaneous, step-level signals under a fixed decoding process. In contrast, whether a token has converged is defined by how its prediction changes
radar.summary_zh: Diffusion language models 透過在所有 tokens 上應用統一的 refinement rule，進行迭代 denoising 來生成文本。然而，實際中 tokens 的穩定速度不同，導致大量的冗餘精煉，並促使對 denoising 過程進行 refinement control。現有方法通常在固定的 decoding process 下，從即時的 step-level signals 評估精煉的必要性。相比之下，一個 token 是否已 converged 取決於其預測如何變化。
radar.title: Progressive Refinement Regulation for Accelerating Diffusion Language Model Decoding
radar.title_zh: 用於加速 Diffusion Language Model 解碼的漸進式精煉調控

links.title: KARL: Knowledge Agents via Reinforcement Learning

primary_link.title: KARL: Knowledge Agents via Reinforcement Learning

top5.summary: We present a system for training enterprise search agents via reinforcement learning that achieves state-of-the-art performance across a diverse suite of hard-to-verify agentic search tasks. Our work makes four core contributions. First, we introduce KARLBench, a multi-capability evaluation suite spanning six distinct search regimes, including constraint-driven entity search, cross-document report synthesis, tabular numerical reasoning, exhaustive entity retrieval, procedural reasoning over technical documentation, and fact aggregation over internal enterprise notes. Second, we show that models trained across heterogeneous search behaviors generalize substantially better than those optimized for any single benchmark. Third, we develop an agentic synthesis pipeline that employs long-horizon reasoning and tool use to generate diverse, grounded, and high-quality training data, with iterative bootstrapping from increasingly capable models. Fourth, we propose a new post-training paradigm based on iterative large-batch off-policy RL that is sample efficient, robust to train-inference engine discrepancies, and naturally extends to multi-task training with out-of-distribution generalization. Compared to Claude 4.6 and GPT 5.2, KARL is Pareto-optimal on KARLBench across cost-quality and latency-quality trade-offs, including tasks that were out-of-distribution during training. With sufficient test-time compute, it surpasses the strongest closed models. These results show that tailored synthetic data in combination with multi-task reinforcement learning enables cost-efficient and high-performing knowledge agents for grounded reasoning.
top5.summary_zh: 我們提出了一個系統，透過強化學習訓練企業搜尋代理（enterprise search agents），在多種難以驗證的代理式搜尋任務（agentic search tasks）中實現了最先進的效能。我們的工作有四個核心貢獻。首先，我們引入了 KARLBench，這是一個多功能評估套件，涵蓋六種不同的搜尋機制，包括受限驅動的實體搜尋（constraint-driven entity search）、跨文件報告合成（cross-document report synthesis）、表格數值推理（tabular numerical reasoning）、詳盡實體檢索（exhaustive entity retrieval）、技術文件上的程序推理（procedural reasoning）以及內部企業筆記的事實聚合（fact aggregation）。其次，我們展示了在異構搜尋行為上訓練的模型，其泛化能力顯著優於針對任何單一基準進行優化的模型。第三，我們開發了一個代理式合成管線（agentic synthesis pipeline），該管線採用長程推理（long-horizon reasoning）和工具使用（tool use）來生成多樣化、有根據且高品質的訓練數據，並透過越來越有能力的模型進行迭代式自我提升（iterative bootstrapping）。第四，我們提出了一種新的後訓練範式（post-training paradigm），該範式基於迭代式大批量離策略強化學習（iterative large-batch off-policy RL），具有樣本效率高、對訓練-推論引擎差異（train-inference engine discrepancies）具有魯棒性，並自然地擴展到具有分佈外泛化能力（out-of-distribution generalization）的多任務訓練。與 Claude 4.6 和 GPT 5.2 相比，KARL 在 KARLBench 上在成本-品質和延遲-品質權衡（cost-quality and latency-quality trade-offs）方面是 Pareto-optimal 的，包括訓練期間屬於分佈外的任務。在足夠的測試時間計算資源下，它超越了最強的閉源模型。這些結果表明，量身定制的合成數據與多任務強化學習相結合，可以為有根據的推理（grounded reasoning）實現高成本效益和高性能的知識代理。
top5.title: KARL: Knowledge Agents via Reinforcement Learning
top5.title_zh: KARL：透過強化學習實現知識代理（Knowledge Agents）

links.title: Helios: Real Real-Time Long Video Generation Model

primary_link.title: Helios: Real Real-Time Long Video Generation Model

top5.summary: We introduce Helios, the first 14B video generation model that runs at 19.5 FPS on a single NVIDIA H100 GPU and supports minute-scale generation while matching the quality of a strong baseline. We make breakthroughs along three key dimensions: (1) robustness to long-video drifting without commonly used anti-drifting heuristics such as self-forcing, error-banks, or keyframe sampling; (2) real-time generation without standard acceleration techniques such as KV-cache, sparse/linear attention, or quantization; and (3) training without parallelism or sharding frameworks, enabling image-diffusion-scale batch sizes while fitting up to four 14B models within 80 GB of GPU memory. Specifically, Helios is a 14B autoregressive diffusion model with a unified input representation that natively supports T2V, I2V, and V2V tasks. To mitigate drifting in long-video generation, we characterize typical failure modes and propose simple yet effective training strategies that explicitly simulate drifting during training, while eliminating repetitive motion at its source. For efficiency, we heavily compress the historical and noisy context and reduce the number of sampling steps, yielding computational costs comparable to -- or lower than -- those of 1.3B video generative models. Moreover, we introduce infrastructure-level optimizations that accelerate both inference and training while reducing memory consumption. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Helios consistently outperforms prior methods on both short- and long-video generation. We plan to release the code, base model, and distilled model to support further development by the community.
top5.summary_zh: 我們介紹了 Helios，這是第一個 14B 影片生成模型，它可以在單一 NVIDIA H100 GPU 上以 19.5 FPS 的速度運行，並支援分鐘級的生成，同時與強大的基準線（baseline）模型保持相同品質。我們在三個關鍵維度上取得了突破性進展：(1) 對長影片漂移（long-video drifting）的魯棒性，無需常用於反漂移的啟發式方法，如 self-forcing、error-banks 或 keyframe sampling；(2) 無需標準加速技術，如 KV-cache、sparse/linear attention 或 quantization，即可實現 real-time generation；以及 (3) 無需 parallelism 或 sharding frameworks 即可進行訓練，從而實現 image-diffusion-scale 的 batch sizes，同時能夠在 80 GB 的 GPU 記憶體中容納多達四個 14B 模型。具體而言，Helios 是一個 14B autoregressive diffusion model，具有統一的輸入表示，原生支援 T2V、I2V 和 V2V 任務。為了減輕長影片生成中的漂移，我們描述了典型的故障模式，並提出了簡單而有效的訓練策略，這些策略在訓練期間明確模擬漂移，同時從根本上消除重複運動。為了提高效率，我們大幅壓縮了歷史和嘈雜的上下文，並減少了 sampling steps 的數量，使計算成本與 1.3B 影片生成模型相當或更低。此外，我們引入了基礎設施級別的優化，這些優化加速了 inference 和 training，同時減少了記憶體消耗。廣泛的實驗表明，Helios 在短影片和長影片生成方面始終優於先前的方法。我們計劃發布 code、base model 和 distilled model，以支持社群的進一步開發。
top5.title: Helios: Real Real-Time Long Video Generation Model
top5.title_zh: Helios：真實即時長影片生成模型

links.title: Feature Resemblance: On the Theoretical Understanding of Analogical Reasoning in Transformers

primary_link.title: Feature Resemblance: On the Theoretical Understanding of Analogical Reasoning in Transformers

top5.summary: Understanding reasoning in large language models is complicated by evaluations that conflate multiple reasoning types. We isolate analogical reasoning (inferring shared properties between entities based on known similarities) and analyze its emergence in transformers. We theoretically prove three key results: (1) Joint training on similarity and attribution premises enables analogical reasoning through aligned representations; (2) Sequential training succeeds only when similarity structure is le
top5.summary_zh: 大型語言模型（large language models）中的推理理解，由於評估將多種推理類型混淆而變得複雜。我們分離出類比推理（analogical reasoning）（根據已知相似性推斷實體之間的共同屬性），並分析其在 transformers 中的出現。我們從理論上證明了三個關鍵結果：(1) 針對相似性和歸因前提（attribution premises）的 Joint training 透過 aligned representations 實現了類比推理；(2) Sequential training 僅在相似性結構為 le 時才能成功。
top5.title: Feature Resemblance: On the Theoretical Understanding of Analogical Reasoning in Transformers
top5.title_zh: Feature Resemblance：關於 Transformer 中類比推理的理論理解

links.title: Memex(RL): Scaling Long-Horizon LLM Agents via Indexed Experience Memory

primary_link.title: Memex(RL): Scaling Long-Horizon LLM Agents via Indexed Experience Memory

top5.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents are fundamentally bottlenecked by finite context windows on long-horizon tasks. As trajectories grow, retaining tool outputs and intermediate reasoning in-context quickly becomes infeasible: the working context becomes prohibitively long, eventually exceeds the context budget, and makes distant evidence harder to use even when it is still present. Existing solutions typically shorten context through truncation or running summaries, but these methods are fundamentally lossy because they compress or discard past evidence itself. We introduce Memex, an indexed experience memory mechanism that instead compresses context without discarding evidence. Memex maintains a compact working context consisting of concise structured summaries and stable indices, while storing full-fidelity underlying interactions in an external experience database under those indices. The agent can then decide when to dereference an index and recover the exact past evidence needed for the current subgoal. We optimize both write and read behaviors with our reinforcement learning framework MemexRL, using reward shaping tailored to indexed memory usage under a context budget, so the agent learns what to summarize, what to archive, how to index it, and when to retrieve it. This yields a substantially less lossy form of long-horizon memory than summary-only approaches. We further provide a theoretical analysis showing the potential of the Memex loop to preserve decision quality with bounded dereferencing while keeping effective in-context computation bounded as history grows. Empirically, on challenging long-horizon tasks, Memex agent trained with MemexRL improves task success while using a significantly smaller working context.
top5.summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）代理在 long-horizon tasks 上受到有限 context windows 的根本限制。隨著 trajectories 的增長，在 in-context 中保留 tool outputs 和 intermediate reasoning 很快變得不可行：working context 變得過長，最終超出 context budget，即使遠處的證據仍然存在，也更難以使用。現有解決方案通常透過 truncation 或 running summaries 來縮短上下文，但這些方法本質上是有損的，因為它們會壓縮或丟棄過去的證據本身。我們引入了 Memex，這是一種索引經驗記憶機制（indexed experience memory mechanism），它在不丟棄證據的情況下壓縮上下文。Memex 維護一個緊湊的 working context，其中包含簡潔的 structured summaries 和 stable indices，同時在這些 indices 下將 full-fidelity 的底層互動儲存在外部 experience database 中。代理隨後可以決定何時 dereference an index 並恢復當前 subgoal 所需的確切過去證據。我們使用我們的 reinforcement learning 框架 MemexRL 優化寫入和讀取行為，採用針對 context budget 下的索引記憶使用量量身定制的 reward shaping，因此代理學習要總結什麼、要歸檔什麼、如何索引以及何時檢索。這產生了一種比純摘要方法損失更少的 long-horizon memory 形式。我們進一步提供了理論分析，顯示 Memex loop 在保持 bounded dereferencing 的情況下保持決策品質的潛力，同時隨著歷史的增長保持有效的 in-context computation 受限。從經驗上看，在具有挑戰性的 long-horizon tasks 上，用 MemexRL 訓練的 Memex 代理在使用顯著更小的 working context 的同時提高了任務成功率。
top5.title: Memex(RL): Scaling Long-Horizon LLM Agents via Indexed Experience Memory
top5.title_zh: Memex(RL)：透過索引經驗記憶擴展長程 LLM 代理

links.title: SlideSparse: Fast and Flexible (2N-2):2N Structured Sparsity

primary_link.title: SlideSparse: Fast and Flexible (2N-2):2N Structured Sparsity

top5.summary: NVIDIA's 2:4 Sparse Tensor Cores deliver 2x throughput but demand strict 50% pruning -- a ratio that collapses LLM reasoning accuracy (Qwen3: 54% to 15%). Milder $(2N-2):2N$ patterns (e.g., 6:8, 25% pruning) preserve accuracy yet receive no hardware support, falling back to dense execution without any benefit from sparsity. We present SlideSparse, the first system to unlock Sparse Tensor Core acceleration for the $(2N-2):2N$ model family on commodity GPUs. Our Sliding Window Decomposition recons
top5.summary_zh: NVIDIA 的 2:4 Sparse Tensor Cores 提供了 2 倍的 throughput，但要求嚴格的 50% pruning——這個比例會導致 LLM reasoning accuracy 急劇下降（Qwen3：從 54% 降至 15%）。較溫和的 $(2N-2):2N$ 模式（例如 6:8，25% pruning）能保持準確性，但卻沒有硬體支援，回歸到 dense execution，無法從 sparsity 中獲益。我們提出了 SlideSparse，這是第一個能在商用 GPU 上為 $(2N-2):2N$ 模型家族解鎖 Sparse Tensor Core 加速的系統。我們的 Sliding Window Decomposition recons
